What is the success rate of otoplasty aka protruding ear surgery aka pinning ear surgery

The success rate of otoplasty, also known as protruding ear surgery or pinning ear surgery, is generally reported to be high. The procedure has been performed for many years and has proven to be safe and effective in repositioning protruding ears closer to the head. According to a study published in the Aesthetic Surgery Journal, the reported success rate for otoplasty was 92.6%, with the majority of patients (85.2%) achieving "excellent" or "good" results. However, as with any surgical procedure, there is always some risk of complications or unsatisfactory results, so it is important for patients to discuss the procedure thoroughly with their surgeon and have realistic expectations for their outcome.

What to ask the doctor if I have ear protrusion surgery after 3 hours

If you are going to have ear protrusion surgery in a few hours, here are some questions you may want to ask your doctor:

1. What will the procedure entail? Can you walk me through the process?
2. What type of anesthesia will be used during the surgery?
3. What are the risks and potential complications associated with this surgery?
4. What type of results can I expect from the surgery and how long will it take for the ears to heal completely?
5. What type of aftercare will be required and will I need to have follow-up appointments?
6. Are there any specific medications or activities I should avoid before or after the surgery?
7. How much time will I need to take off work or school after surgery?
8. Can you show me before-and-after photos of previous patients who underwent this surgery?
9. How much experience do you have performing this procedure?
10. What is the total cost of the surgery, and will my insurance cover any of it?

Sure, here are some additional questions you may want to ask your doctor after ear protrusion surgery:

1. How long will I need to wear a bandage or compression garment over my ears after surgery?
2.What can I do to help manage pain or discomfort in the days after surgery?
3. When can I return to my normal activities and exercise routines after surgery?
4. Are there any specific complications I should be aware of and how can I recognize them if they occur?
5. Can I still wear hats or other headwear after the surgery and, if so, when can I start to do so?
6. Should I be doing anything specific to care for my ears during the healing process?
7. Is there a possibility that additional surgery might be needed in the future?
8. When will I be able to see the final results of the surgery?
9. Will my ears look natural after the protrusion surgery?
10. What are some realistic expectations I should have for the outcome of the surgery?
